rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes
Use valid component type scope for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Preserve existing subcomponents and connections when adding implementation-level bindings
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when inserting annex blocks
Preserve existing model elements during fusion
Use valid scope for each annex block type
Ensure inserted annex blocks are syntactically correct
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes
Do not mix component type and implementation annex blocks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
